ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2019 Off Calendar

ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-02-2018 Continued To Aug-01-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants California Shipbuilding Dry Dock Company, And Marine Engineering And Supply Company
Off Calendar Nov-21-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Michael Espinosa, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Velma McAllister, who was not present at today's hearing.
No appearance by defaulted defendants California Shipbuilding & Dry Dock Company or Marine Engineering and Supply Company.
Default was entered against defendant California Shipbuilding & Dry Dock Company on May 10, 2016.
Default was entered against defendant Marine Engineering & Supply Company on October 31, 2016.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on November 20, 2017.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Velma McAllister, and against the Defaulted Defendants California Shipbuilding & Dry Dock Company and Marine Engineering and Supply Company, as follows:

Defendant California Shipbuilding & Dry Dock Company
Economic Damages - $84,250.00.
Non-Economic Damages - $750,000.00.
Total - $834,250.00.

Defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company
Economic Damages - $84,250.00.
Non-Economic Damages - $750,000.00.
Total - $834,250.00.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-08-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Michael C. Guasco of YARON & ASSOCIATES (415.658.2929), appearing for defendants /moving party Puget Sound Commerce Center, Inc., formerly known as Todd Shipyards Corporation.

Patrick R. Campbell of BRAYTON PURCELL LLP (415.898.1555), appearing for plaintiff/opposing party Velma McAllister.

RULING:
DEFEENDANT PUGET SOUND COMMERCE CENTER, INC., formerly known as TODD SHIPYARDS CORPORATION NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT.

Argument held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling: Defendant Puget Sound Commerce Center, Inc.'s special demurrer to plaintiff's fourth cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff's fourth cause of action alleges sufficient facts to meet the standards set forth in Campbell v. Ford Motor Co., (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 15. Defendant has 10 days to answer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Diana L. Gonzalez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #7935 (Tel: 510-451-1580). (503/TLJ)
